( a ) The United Nations and other international organizations, international or regional financial institutions and donor agencies involved in disaster risk reduction, as appropriate, shall strengthen the coordination of their strategies in this regard;

(b) The entities of the United Nations system, including funds, programmes and specialized agencies, within the framework of the United Nations Plan of Action for Disaster Risk Reduction for Resilience Building, the United Nations Development Assistance Frameworks and country programmes, call for the best possible use of resources and support to developing countries, upon request, in the implementation of the present Framework, in coordination with other relevant frameworks, such as the International Health Regulations (2005), including through development and capacity-building, and through clear and targeted programmes that respond to the priorities of States, in a balanced, well-coordinated and sustainable manner;

(c) The United Nations Office for Disaster Reduction, in particular, shall support the implementation, follow-up and review of this Framework, including through the preparation of periodic reports on progress made, in particular for the Global Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction and, as appropriate, following the schedule of the United Nations follow-up process; by promoting the establishment of monitoring mechanisms and the development of coherent global and regional indicators on sustainable development goals, in coordination, as appropriate, with other relevant mechanisms focused on sustainable development and climate change; by updating the Hyogo Framework for Action Implementation Monitoring System; by actively participating in the work of the United Nations and external expert group on indicators related to sustainable development goals; by developing, for the purpose of implementation, practical guidelines for disaster risk reduction, including disaster risk reduction, including disaster risk reduction platforms and disaster risk reduction platforms, including disaster risk reduction platforms, and disaster risk information and outreach; by strengthening the prevention culture among relevant stakeholders and by promoting, for that purpose, the development of standards by experts and disaster risk reduction platforms, including disaster risk reduction platforms, including disaster risk reduction platforms and disaster risk reduction platforms, including disaster risk reduction platforms, as well as well as well as well as disaster risk-related and disaster risk-related platforms, as well as as well as well as well as well as well as well as well as well as well as well as well as relevant disaster risk information information

( d ) International financial institutions, such as the World Bank and regional development banks, shall review the priorities of this Framework with a view to providing financial support and lending to developing countries for integrated disaster risk reduction;

(e) Other international organizations and treaty bodies, including the Conference of the Parties to the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, international financial institutions at the global and regional levels and the International Red Cross and Red Crescent Movement, assist developing countries, upon request, in the implementation of this Framework, in coordination with other relevant frameworks;

(f) As the main United Nations initiative on collaboration with the private sector and the business community, the Global Compact continues to do so, raising awareness among stakeholders of the importance of risk reduction for sustainable development and resilience;

(g) The overall capacity of the United Nations system to assist developing countries in disaster risk reduction is strengthened by providing the necessary resources through various funding mechanisms, including increased, stable and predictable timely financial contributions to the United Nations Trust Fund for Disaster Reduction, and by enhancing the role of the Fund in the implementation of the present Framework;

( h ) The Inter-Parliamentary Union and other relevant regional bodies and mechanisms, as appropriate, continue to campaign for disaster risk reduction and the strengthening of national legal frameworks;

(i) United Cities and Local Governments and other relevant local government bodies continue to encourage cooperation and mutual education among local governments in order to reduce disaster risk and implement this Framework.

text_fr: Mesures de suivi

items_fr: 49. La  Conférence  invite  l'Assemblée  générale  à  envisager  d'inclure,  à  sa soixante-dixième session, l'examen des progrès accomplis au niveau mondial dans l'application  du  Cadre  de  Sendai  pour  la  réduction  des  risques  de  catastrophe (2015-2030)  dans  le  suivi  intégré  et  coordonné  des  textes  issus  des  grandes conférences  et  réunions  au  sommet  organisées  par  les  Nations  Unies,  en coordination  avec  le  Conseil  économique  et  social,  le  Forum  politique  de  haut niveau pour le développement durable et les cycles d'examen quadriennal complet, selon qu'il conviendra, compte tenu des contributions de la Plateforme mondiale pour  la  réduction  des  risques  de  catastrophe  et  des  plateformes  régionales  de réduction des risques de catastrophe, ainsi que du système de suivi de la mise en œuvre du Cadre d'action de Hyogo.

50.  La Conférence recommande à l'Assemblée générale de créer, à sa soixanteneuvième  session,  un  groupe  de  travail  intergouvernemental  à  composition  non limitée, composé d'experts nommés par les États Membres et appuyé par le Bureau des  Nations  Unies  pour  la  prévention  des  catastrophes,  auquel  participeront  les parties  prenantes  concernées,  qui  sera  chargé  d'établir  une  série  d'indicateurs permettant de mesurer les progrès accomplis à l'échelle mondiale dans la mise en
items_en: 49. The Conference invites the General Assembly to consider including, at its seventieth session, the review of global progress in the implementation of the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction (2015-2030) in the integrated and coordinated follow-up to the outcomes of the major United Nations conferences and summits, in coordination with the Economic and Social Council, the High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development and the quadrennial comprehensive policy review cycles, as appropriate, taking into account the contributions of the Global Platform for Disaster Risk Reduction and the regional platforms for disaster risk reduction, as well as the system for monitoring the implementation of the Hyogo Framework for Action.

50. The Conference recommends that the General Assembly, at its sixty-ninth session, establish an open-ended intergovernmental working group, composed of experts nominated by Member States and supported by the United Nations Office for Disaster Reduction, with the participation of relevant stakeholders, to develop a set of indicators to measure global progress in the implementation of the Millennium Development Goals.

Elle  recommande  également  que  le  groupe  de  travail examine les recommandations du Groupe consultatif scientifique et technique du Bureau en ce qui concerne l'actualisation de la publication intitulée « Terminologie 2009 pour la prévention des risques de catastrophe » d'ici à décembre 2016, et que les  conclusions  de  ses  travaux  soient  soumises  à  l'Assemblée  pour  examen  et adoption.
